mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 8% over 3 months signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the face of a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 500,000 units signals a strengthening demand for housing that boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the face of a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 90 signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profits and boosts borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the face of a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that boosts discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising federal funds rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the face of a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 3% signals a strengthening demand for goods and services that boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising real GDP are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the face of a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in initial unemployment claims below 300,000 signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with declining initial unemployment claims are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the face of a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 3% signals a strengthening demand for goods and services that boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising real disposable personal income are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the face of a recession.
